# keel

> Scaffold a new git repository from composable template modules, then optionally
> create and push the remote — one self-contained Go binary.

## Commands
- keel new: scaffold a repo from a recipe (interactive or --answers/--no-input).
- keel update: re-apply evolved module templates to an existing repo.
- keel outdated: report modules with newer versions.
- keel list / config / version.

## Concepts
- Recipe: a named composition of modules (go-service, rust-service, or a user file).
- Module: module.yaml manifest + templates/ tree; questions + files (with when-conditions).
- Providers: github, gitlab, bitbucket, sourcecraft (tokens from env), or none.
- .scaffold.lock records recipe/modules/answers + per-file hashes for keel update.

## Docs
- Site: https://romanagaltsev.github.io/keel/
- Repo: https://github.com/RomanAgaltsev/keel
- Examples: https://github.com/RomanAgaltsev/keel/tree/main/examples
